para.heading: Issues
Can't save a password

listitem.para: Is the folder initialized? Easiest way is to use the [Users] button and make sure you can encrypt for someone (e.g. yourself)
Are you using Git? If not, make sure it is switched off.

para.heading: I have an issue with GNOME keyring

listitem.para: Disable GNOME keyring
Create a ~/.gnupg/gpg-agent.conf containing:
See the pass FAQ for more troubleshooting tips.

para.heading: I don't get a passphrase / PIN dialog

listitem.para: You'll need to install a pinentry package (often called pinentry-qt, pinentry-qt5, or a similar name like pinentry-gtk depending on your distribution; search in your package manager) and possibly set the full path to that executable in your ~/.gnupg/gpg-agent.conf for example: pinentry-program /usr/bin/pinentry-qt
On some systems it might be necessary to create a symbolic link /usr/bin/pinentry to your pinentry application of choice eg: ln -s /usr/bin/pinentry-qt /usr/bin/pinentry
On macOS pinentry-program /usr/local/bin/pinentry-mac works after installing pinentry-mac from Homebrew.

para.heading: I have another issue with gpg

listitem.para: On modern systems, gpg is usually GnuPG 2.x already. If your system has separate gpg and gpg2 keyrings (typically older or custom setups), your key may exist in one but not the other.

detaileddescription.para: Where [ID] is your gpg key-id. If your setup is reversed, exchange gpg and gpg2 accordingly.

para.heading: Git doesn't work on Windows

detaileddescription.para: Git for Windows comes with an ssh-askpass compatible command, git-gui--askpass (located in /mingw64/libexec/git-core/git-gui--askpass on PortableGit version, presumably some place similar for the installed version).
To use it:

listitem.para: Set SSH_ASKPASS environment variable to the full path of git-gui--askpass, for example:
Optionally set GIT_ASKPASS to the same value so Git uses the same helper.
Restart QtPass after setting the environment variables.

detaileddescription.para: Example using PowerShell:
Or to make it permanent:
Note: After using setx, you'll need to start QtPass in a new session (close and reopen your terminal, or log out and back in) for the environment variables to take effect.

para.heading: Git has issues with GPG SSH Authentication

detaileddescription.para: This tutorial might resolve your issues. https://gitforwindows.org/OpenSSH-Integration-with-Pageant

para.heading: GPG says "Public key unusable" or "No secret key"

detaileddescription.para: Your GPG key may not be trusted or has expired. Run gpg --edit-key <KEYID> and:

listitem.para: Set the trust level to "ultimate" (trust command)
Check if the key has expired (expire command to extend if needed)
For smartcards/YubiKeys, ensure the card is connected and unlocked (you can verify with gpg --card-status)

para.heading: What is the "Signing Key" in profile settings?

detaileddescription.para: The Signing Key field in profile configuration is optional and usually should be left empty.
It is only needed if you want to create detached signatures for your .gpg-id files to verify the recipients list hasn't been tampered with. Most users can ignore this field.
Common mistake: Putting your GPG key ID here will cause "Signature does not exist" errors when saving passwords. Leave it empty unless you specifically need signature verification.

para.heading: OTP QR codes don't work on macOS

detaileddescription.para: Applications launched from Finder don't inherit the shell PATH. Install QtPass via Homebrew (which sets up PATH correctly) or create a wrapper script that sets PATH before launching QtPass, for example:

Save this script (e.g. as ~/bin/qtpass-wrapper.sh), make it executable with chmod +x ~/bin/qtpass-wrapper.sh, and configure your launcher (Automator app, custom .app bundle, or shortcut) to run this script instead of starting QtPass directly.

para.heading: QtPass doesn't follow my system language

detaileddescription.para: QtPass uses the system language at startup. On some desktop environments, you may need to launch QtPass from the terminal or configure your desktop environment to pass the correct locale environment variables.

para.heading: Where is the configuration stored?

detaileddescription.para: QtPass tries to use the native configuration location for the operating system it is running on.

listitem.para: Linux and BSD: $HOME/.config/IJHack/QtPass.conf
macOS: $HOME/Library/Preferences/com.IJHack.QtPass.plist
Windows registry: HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\IJHack\QtPass

detaileddescription.para: These settings can be overridden by a qtpass.ini file in the folder where the application resides. So-called "portable config".
There are some things to take care of when trying to sync on some systems (especially macOS, with regards to text and binary .plist files).
More information: https://doc.qt.io/qt-6/qsettings.html#platform-specific-notes

para.heading: Where can I ask for help?

listitem.para: Create an issue on GitHub.
Send an email to help@qtpass.org
Visit the Official QtPass site

para.heading: Can I import from KeePass, LastPass or X?

listitem.para: Yes, check passwordstore.org/#migration for more info.

para.heading: I don't see icons on the buttons

detaileddescription.para: You do not have the Qt SVG library installed. Please install using your favorite package manager.

para.heading: I get icons that do not fit my (X11) default

listitem.para: On some WindowManagers, Qt doesn't know what icon set to use. A trick:

para.heading: Can not find either qmake or qmake6

detaileddescription.para: QtPass uses Qt6 by default. Use:

listitem.para: qmake6 - for Qt6 (recommended, default)
qmake - for Qt5

detaileddescription.para: If you get an error like "QApplication" not found or Qt installation issues, make sure you're using the correct qmake version for your Qt installation.
On some systems, you may need to specify the full path, e.g., /usr/lib/qt6/bin/qmake6.

para.heading: Qt installation issues

detaileddescription.para: If you encounter Qt installation issues, you'll have to install the current version via your package manager or download it from https://www.qt.io/download/ and build from source.

para.heading: I don't like the design, what gives?

listitem.para: It's all on GitHub, clone, change and send a pull request.
Open an issue and point out defects or better yet propose changes.

para.heading: QtPass is not in my native language

listitem.para: Unfortunately, QtPass might not support your native language, or the translations might be incomplete. Check if newer versions of QtPass support it.
If translations are available but aren't working, try to set the language manually (see below) or open an issue.

para.heading: How do I set the language manually?

detaileddescription.para: QtPass uses the system language. Changing it depends on your system:

listitem.para: on Linux: LANGUAGE=fr qtpass will run QtPass in French.

para.heading: How can I help improve QtPass?
I would like to donate

listitem.para:
Time:
Read contributing documentation.
Fork, clone, hack and send a pull request.
Find an issue to work on.
Participate in our bug bounty, you submit an issue and help us fix it, I send you a bounty.
